data.narrative:
Isar Aerospace, a Series C space & aerospace company that has raised $650M+ to date, is valued at $1.0B+ on a post-money basis. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Loft Orbital) and five public reference companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two quantitative methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier from the $1.0B last post-money valuation, yielding $1.7B. Peer-median anchoring across the four same-stage comparables produced $1.3B; notably, the peer set ranges from $1.0B to $8.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in the sector. Public-comp anchoring was not applied due to the absence of target revenue data, though the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 36.9x P/S. These two applicable methods support a range of $1.1B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median implication of $1.5B.

Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set and reliance on post-money valuation as the baseline. The high end ($2.0B) reflects stage-progression momentum; the low end ($1.1B) aligns with the current market signal and median peer positioning. Without disclosed revenue or near-term capital deployment plans, upside scenarios remain speculative.
